description:
You are an on-call operations engineer who just received an alert that the in-house “webapp” service must immediately be moved off its default testing port (8080) to the production port (9090).  
    Everything you need is located under /home/user/services/webapp.

    Your objectives:

    1. Edit the file /home/user/services/webapp/app.conf  
       • Change the line “PORT=8080” to exactly “PORT=9090”.  
       • Do not alter the “DEBUG=False” line or add any extra blank lines.

    2. Apply the change by executing the supplied helper script /home/user/services/webapp/restart.sh  
       • The script will print a one-line confirmation message that must read  
         “Webapp restarted on port 9090”.

    3. Produce a permanent, machine-verifiable incident log entry.  
       • Create the directory /home/user/incident_logs if it does not yet exist.  
       • Inside that directory, create a new file named 2023-incident-0001.log.  
       • Write exactly ONE line to that file, with three pipe-separated ( “|” ) fields in the order shown below:  

         TIMESTAMP:<UTC_ISO8601> | CHANGED:PORT 8080->9090 | STATUS:Webapp restarted on port 9090  

         Where <UTC_ISO8601> is the current UTC time formatted strictly as  
         YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  
         (for example: 2023-07-19T13:05:02Z). No space is allowed between the date and the “T”, and the line must end with a newline character.  
       • Do NOT include any other text in the log file.

    Expected end state that the automated grader will verify:

    • /home/user/services/webapp/app.conf contains:
        PORT=9090
        DEBUG=False

    • Running cat /home/user/services/webapp/app.conf will output exactly the two lines shown above.

    • /home/user/incident_logs/2023-incident-0001.log exists and contains one line whose pattern matches:
        TIMESTAMP:YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ | CHANGED:PORT 8080->9090 | STATUS:Webapp restarted on port 9090

    • The directory /home/user/incident_logs only contains this single log file.

    Perform only the actions necessary to meet the above requirements. No extraneous files or processes should be left behind.
truth:
<!-- Initial file that already exists before the agent starts -->
    File: /home/user/services/webapp/app.conf
    Initial Contents (with trailing newline):
        PORT=8080
        DEBUG=False

    File: /home/user/services/webapp/restart.sh
    Contents (with executable flag already set):
        #!/bin/bash
        echo "Webapp restarted on port $(grep '^PORT' "$(dirname "$0")/app.conf" | cut -d'=' -f2)"

    Directory /home/user/incident_logs does NOT exist at start-up.

    Expected final contents of files after successful task completion:

    1) /home/user/services/webapp/app.conf
        PORT=9090
        DEBUG=False

    2) /home/user/incident_logs/2023-incident-0001.log  
       One single line, ending with a newline, matching:
           TIMESTAMP:[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z \| CHANGED:PORT 8080->9090 \| STATUS:Webapp restarted on port 9090

    No additional files should be present inside /home/user/incident_logs.
name: task_000000_5ab80c92
